stories.title: Democratic Senate candidates clash over data centers in latest debate
stories.dek: U.S. Rep. Haley Stevens and Abdul El-Sayed offered contrasting views on data center development during the Michigan U.S. Senate debate, with clips highlighting affordability concerns and corporate influence.

stories.title: El-Sayed outlines strict conditions for data centers in Michigan Senate debate
stories.dek: Democratic candidate Abdul El-Sayed called for union labor requirements, no utility rate hikes, water protections, and enforceable community benefits agreements during the first one-on-one debate with Haley Stevens.

stories.title: Resident notes scale of Saline data center during drive
stories.dek: A Michigan resident described the Saline data center as rivaling Michigan International Speedway in size while passing the site.

stories.title: GPUS advances AI data center plans at Dowagiac campus with major service agreement
stories.dek: The company detailed a 20 MW AI data center deal potentially worth $1.2 billion, with expansion options up to 52 MW at its Michigan site.

stories.title: Saline Township to hold public hearing on Oracle data center tax exemption
stories.dek: The board will consider an Industrial Facilities Tax Exemption Certificate for Oracle America's $43 billion project near Ann Arbor on July 8.

stories.title: John James faces scrutiny over data center builder donations
stories.dek: The Republican U.S. Senate candidate criticized data centers while having accepted over $100,000 from executives and relatives tied to a major Michigan data center builder, according to a Detroit Metro Times report shared on X.

stories.title: Hayes Township Data Center Advisory Group meets to review report draft
stories.dek: The group convenes July 8 to assess proposed report structure and plan next steps on local data center issues.

stories.title: Michigan grid strains raise questions about data center power costs
stories.dek: Recent heat and storms exposed limited slack in the state's electricity system as large data centers approach, prompting discussion on who will pay for upgrades.

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.body: Hayes Township Data Center Advisory Group
meetings.topic: Review draft report structure and schedule next meeting on data center issues
